item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: loved the backstory and loved everything! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An ancient bronze reality-warping mecha with nuclear weapons parallels? I must commend you for introducing Numidium to the wiki without making it obtrusive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This scip references so many things I love: Bronze Age Cultures, ancient technology, lost history, linguistics, etc., and you woven it together with an article written with a professional tone that reveals enough of the narrative to delight without it crossing the line into a tale. Take my upvote, take all my upvotes!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article cannot be sourced, and thus it is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, I've removed the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have mixed feelings about how the description is full of technical details that seem meant to show how it could have been built and powered non-anomalously. On the one hand, it's fun to go research the power output of natural reactors, the tensile strenght of copper alloys, the air pressure necessary to breath through a tube of yea diameter by yea length. On the other hand…fuck it, these folks got a real, if broken, god, let's say the strength of their belief is really what keeps the thing going.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Paragraphs 4 and 5 of the description are the perfect way to write a battle in SCP article format, I felt like I was there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got Damn I do enjoy a new find when browsing SCP artwork! Nice SCP, love me some last ditch efforts lost in a war we don't remember.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: {nevermind}

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing wrong about this SCP is that it's on a wiki that doesn't allow multiple upvotes.

+1e42

I submit myself to the wrath of moderation for this content-free post. Consider it an unofficial second upvote.

edit - just a note, I was led here by a trail of SCPs with 1050 in it, was going to mention it before I saw LittleSallyDigby's post above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha! Well crap, I thought there was one but I couldn't remember which it was. I'll pretend that 1050 was discovered after 2406 (since I like to imagine that she would have access being a major language expert) - I know that not every SCP has to sync up but I do prefer to not totally contradict others ;p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Note for other linguistics/cryptography nerds: the Foundation already has a translation for Linear A, which they're suppressing due to the source: SCP-1050. Presumably Dr. Low doesn't have clearance for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how the possible translation of Linear A is considered to be merely "incidental".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this one both as stand alone and part of bigger picture.
I also this strange urge to make it into a playable unit under BT construction rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written and researched! What I liked the most was the attention to detail as to how the drivers (riders?) of the Colossi would be nurtured and maintained while doing their fighting!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As mecha lover, I love this thing.

Damn, I wonder if the left arm was really torn-off? It might actually be last ditch effort to take down the bastard with rocket punch…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Giant robot could go wrong so many ways, successfully avoiding those traps without making it sound hokey is impressive on its own. Not hard to figure out why Sarkicsm is becoming my new favorite (sub?)GoI.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been doing some research for a Sarkicism/CotBG Nexus I'm writing, and I noticed a plothole in this. It's said that one of the documents recovered was the Book of Pieces Chapter 12, linking to The Broken Mind, but the content of The Broken Mind implies that it's a relatively recently created text, made long after when SCP-2406 was active, what with its references to "Acolyte Barnabus Smythe" and "Sons of Abraham". It's a cool reference, but you might want to remove it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
http://orig06.deviantart.net/ccf6/f/2015/226/4/a/scp_2406_by_sunnyclockwork-d95q23c.jpg

An image of 2406 doing battle by sunnyclockwork:)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not too far from the truth. SCP-835 has a *lot* in common with Sarkic abominations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Based on the description of the damage, I can't help but imagine that this was fighting a version of SCP-835 when it got rendered inoperable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad you changed your mind. It would have cheapened the work, I think. And this is anything but "cheap".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any relation between the flesh god and 610 the flesh that hates?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is super epic. Well well well done. I need to go start reading up on Mesopotamian deities and other ancient beleifs.

This reminded me more of evangelion than any other giant robots, and the Thaumiel SCP-2406-1 as similar to the spear of Longinus. Also man oh man I hope this thing gets repaired and put into use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had originally considered having a request for personnel to stop referring to SCP-2406 as "Voltron" or "insert favorite mecha here" ;p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hopefully this looks better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've gotta say, the positioning of that image is really bugging the hell out of me. However, the rest of this is just ridiculously cool, and adds pretty well to the mythos you've been building up lately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N3CQfvfv1sI

A song to listen to while you imagine yourself in your own colossus doing battle with flesh-fiends.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
click-click-click
Hear that? That is the sound of all the stuff you've been writing finally falling into place in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. Will fix that. Was one of my worries as well while writing that. I wanted a well researched, fully detailed explanation of the technology involved, while also touching on the esoteric and mystical. Often a hard thing to balance while also keeping a realistic format for the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this to death. It was a bit of a ride back and forth, as every once in a while I felt a little taken out of it, but ultimately it is completely awesome and worth the full read.

One minor point: I noticed a few other grammatical things, but the one that stuck out was in the containment procedures, Kazakhstan military base should be Kazakhstani military base. Just saying the country name sounds weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I admit, I worried the word "merchant" might imply dark. Was more to suggest Ion's selfish motives, more a middle-man between eldritch horrors and this world, rather than a worshiper of said horrors. I do refer to Dark in my Alagadda SCP. Where, all names translate as titles of sort, and is thus referenced in passing as the "Deathless Merchant of London" - if I recall. Much like Ion, I do view Dark as the sort willing to sell out the world to get what he wants;p

Edit: Also altered it to say the left arm was not discovered initially with the rest of SCP-2406 and to see the addendum for details.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a reference to Grand Karcist Ion, a recurring character in some of Metaphysician's skips. He's a former half-Daevite slave that made a deal with the flesh-god YALDABAOTH (basically the Demiurge from Gnosticism) and lead a revolt from the Daevite city of Adytum, which lead to Adytum being removed from this version of reality. Now both he and Adytum are something… else.

Though you've convinced me to modify my headcanon so that MC&D are somehow connected to the Sarkic cults.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The left arm appears to have been torn from SCP-2406 and has yet to be recovered.

Addendum: On December 12, 1998, the left arm of SCP-2406 was uncovered 32 km from its body. Evidence of impact suggest the object had been hurled to its location shortly after being severed from SCP-2406's torso. The arm is equipped with what has since been hypothesized to be a weapon of non-terrestrial origins and anomalous manufacture, design, and capabilities. Due to its singular nature when compared to the rest of SCP-2406, this weapon has been separately classified as SCP-2406-1.

Since we now have the left arm, why don't you have have a reference to the addendum next to the top part so people can know it has been found?

Edit: Also,
Spoilers?
– hide block

Upon a throne of black ambition, the Enemy plots. The Enemy is not a priest. They are a merchant. And they have sold the [world/whole/totality].

Is this ancient Mr. Dark?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great, now I'll have to change my giant-related idea so it doesn't seem similar. Not that I can make it even remotely as good as this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've seen drafts of this and I liked it then and I like it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Blood Empress is a reference to *the* Daeva herself. Who, I mean, one on one could be a threat to Ion perhaps. Both are players in a larger game, and Ion is a sort of usurper. Both rely on anomalous objects to augment themselves. Ion steals his initial relics from the Daeva, looting their reliquaries as he triggers revolts. He doesn't conquer all regions under their influence but does end up controlling a sizable former Daevite territory… before *something* happened, lost to history - causing Ion to pull away from the world and the Daeva to vanish. And in its wake, the chaos that brought the Bronze Age world into darkness.

Adytum is the city-state, formerly loyal to the Daeva, where Ion begins his revolution and his cult.

Of course, all of these are presented from the biased accounts of different cults. Dracula Factory is biased in favor of Ion, the villagers obviously holding him in reverence. Actual history but cloaked in legend.

The Red Death is a type of plague, based on the Red Death of Poe's Masque of the Red Death. Ancient biological warfare ;p

Edit: I'm actually going to touch on Sarkicsm with a Foundation document soon. I want to keep things open enough so that other writers can build it up as well, just like the CotBG and other GOI. It will be written by Dr. Judith Low and be a sort of "What we know so far" document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We need to establish power levels here. It's odd that we are more curious about the Sarkic menace than the CotBG (which this article should be centred around).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would doubt it. The Archons are, I'd assume, like second only to YALDABAOTH in terms of power. I'd assume that would mean they're more powerful than Ion, and the Blood Empress is explicitly not as powerful as Ion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On that note, is the Blood Empress in Dracula Factory considered as among the Archons?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you implying that 610 (The Red Death summoned at Gyaros) is an Archon? Or have I got that completely wrong?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given that they're both Aegean in origin a connection would make sense. I think this is more related to Talos though.

EDIT: Called it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, but it is related to http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Talos

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my mind, the real threat is with the Orthodoxy. I imagine the church has been in a fallen state for quite some time, but at one point, represented a collection of the greatest thinkers of the ancient world - engineers, scientists, philosophers, alchemists, and so on.

I'm thinking, with some of these ancient threats returning, many in the Church are trying to return to what they once were as well. I would like to explore something involving the Clockwork Orthodoxy, who to me - seem bent on freezing time with the 1700s-1800s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Archons! 2510 hints to Archons - attendants to the blind idiot god.

"Now I slept in the thought of my heart. […] YALDABAOTH6 […] blind. The Enemy […] Sarkic Legions. ADYTUM, a city older than […] - a place of terrible, unnumbered crimes. […] as long as the Archons remain unbound. […] ION THE BETRAYER. […] THE DEATHLESS. […] CRIMSON […] HORNED BRUTE. ASTAPHE, EMPRESS OF [..]. The great and powerful Archon, who is full of anger, the successor of the Archon of the outer darkness, the place in which all forms change, [..] who is spread out upon the way of the Midst, who carries off the souls by theft.")"

And in Gnosticism, Archons tend to come in six:) In my mind, one such archon is related to the destruction of Crete, summoned by the Minoan Conspirators hinted to in a previous SCP of mine (Gyaros). But I forgot all about writing it;p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: my first thought when I saw the title was that it would be related to the Colossus at Rhodes. Still like. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who is really into Mesopotamian mythology, I am now imagining Mummu, Kingu, Nammu and Abzu as Flesh-deities of some sort.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've wanted to do something with Apophis for some time actually;p The *thing* this machine and crew were sent to fight was a bit inspired by Typhon/Echinda.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like how the CoTBG is slowly becoming one of the few "good" guys in the world. They're no longer just a weird cult, they're a complicated religion that is actively fighting the coming apocalypse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh God, now I'm imagining giant monsters rising from the Mediterranean… Maybe that's where things like Typhon or Apep came from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fun fact: the entity that left it in its current state. The CotBG would probably describe it as a "hydra".

Modern observers would likely say the saw something like that in AKIRA.

SCP-XXXX-1 was also indeed used and is still "locked" on its target.

Technically, SCP-XXXX-1 is Thaumiel and the Foundation really *really* shouldn't mess with it much ;p Less they undo its last action.

I also admit, I never saw Pacific Rim. My inspiration for this was a) the mythical Talos and b) a giant, ancient automaton from a game I played a lot in my youth which is depicted in this intro video :) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DW6iW8EitVs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Yessssss. I'm not sure whether I should do it or let a better writer have it though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heh. I like giant robots and I like nuclear physics.
I also liked Pacific Rim.

This works for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude. Dude. Resurrection

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Metaphysician, why you so epic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like well-researched references to history. I like attempts to tie various bits of SCP lore together. I like mythological beings that are analyzed and written up as SCPs in a "here's how it could have plausibly happened" way. I like giant robots. Everything is here. +1.

Also…

SCP-2406 is currently disabled (but could hypothetically be repaired)

You do realize that this tale definitely needs to be written now, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost included a request by the Site Director for researchers to stop calling it "Voltron";p (or the fictional giant mecha of their choice)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did you just-

And it-

But that-

It's-

This gave me the headcanon equivalent of an erection.

PLUS ONE SO HARD EVERY GLASS SURFACE IN EARSHOT JUST EXPLODED.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MEDITERRANEAN RIM

We are cancelling the Sarkic Dawn.
